Sara Hassan is a Web and Graphics Designer with contact information including her email (sara.hassan@email.com), phone number (+20 101 234 5678), and links to her GitHub and LinkedIn profiles, as well as her personal website (sarahassan.com).
Sara Hassan obtained a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Nagpur University in September 2015, located in Nagpur, Maharashtra, India.
A web-based expert system was developed as a final year project from 2014 to 2015, providing online disease consultation and improving access to medical advice.
A Shopping Management System was developed using C++ from 2013 to 2014, including functionalities for product inventory, sales, and customer transactions.
The individual possesses basic skills in C and JAVA, and proficiency in web technologies like HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and jQuery.
The individual is skilled in graphics design tools such as Corel Draw, Adobe Photoshop, and Adobe Illustrator, and utilizes MySQL 5.0 for databases.
The individual uses IDEs and tools like Sublime Text and Notepad, and is familiar with operating systems Windows XP and Windows 7.
In 2015, the individual coordinated a project competition as part of FACE-IT, demonstrating leadership and organizational skills.
In 2015, the individual presented at the Project Competition at Priyadarshini Indira Gandhi College of Engineering, Nagpur, as part of Innovesta 15.
In 2015, the individual presented a paper at the National Level Paper Presentation in TECH-WAVE 2015 held at S.R.M.C.E., Nagpur.

From September 2017 to August 2019, Sara Hassan worked as a full-time Web and Graphics Designer at Virtuous Media Point, utilizing skills in HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, jQuery, Corel Draw, Photoshop, and Illustrator to create web and graphic designs and collaborating with clients.

From September 2015 to August 2017, Sara Hassan was a Web Designing and Development Intern/Web Designer at e-sense IT Solution Pvt. Ltd., where she designed website solutions, gathered requirements, prepared specifications, participated in the SDLC and Agile methodology, and developed and debugged code.

Between 2018 and 2019, Sara Hassan developed and maintained administrative panels for web projects including www.nitka.com, Nagpur united corporation, Mintmetrix.com, Tagline videos, and Smartbadge, using HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and jQuery with Sublime Text.

From 2017 to 2018, Sara Hassan designed and developed responsive websites for clients such as www.shreekiaspack.co.in, www.3staragroproducts.com, and www.luckystationery.co.in, using HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, and JavaScript with Notepad++.
Between 2017 and 2019, Sara Hassan designed various graphic assets including Logos, Brochures, Advertising Banners, Visiting Cards, Pamphlets, and Hoardings.

Nagpur is a city pulsing with academic energy, a hub for aspiring engineers and innovators in Maharashtra, India. It’s a place where potential takes form within the walls of institutions like Priyadarshini Indira Gandhi College of Engineering and S.R.M.C.E., both cradled within its limits. The city nurtures talent, providing a platform for students to showcase their work – a stage like Innovesta 15’s Project Competition, and forums such as the National Level Paper Presentation at TECH-WAVE 2015. Nagpur University, the city’s academic cornerstone, consistently produces graduates ready to contribute to the technological landscape. In September 2015, Sara Hassan emerged from its ranks, earning a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology, a testament to Nagpur’s commitment to fostering a skilled and forward-thinking generation. The city isn’t merely a location; it’s an incubator, a breeding ground for ideas and a launchpad for careers.

Sara Hassan is a versatile web and graphics designer, a digital architect with a keen eye for both form and function. Graduating with a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Nagpur University in 2015, she quickly transitioned from an internship at e-sense IT Solution Pvt. Ltd. – where she honed her skills in website development, requirements gathering, and agile methodologies – into a full-fledged role at Virtuous Media Point. 

There, from 2017 to 2019, she became proficient in crafting compelling online experiences, expertly wielding HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, jQuery, Photoshop, Illustrator, and Corel Draw to meet diverse client needs. She doesn’t just build websites; she builds solutions, demonstrated by her work developing and maintaining administrative panels for projects like nitka.com and Nagpur united corporation. 

Her portfolio showcases a talent for responsive design, evident in the websites she created for Shreekiaspack.co.in, 3staragroproducts.com, and Luckystationery.co.in. Beyond the digital realm, Sara’s creative reach extends to traditional graphic design, encompassing logos, brochures, advertising banners, and a range of marketing collateral.  She’s a pragmatic designer, comfortable with tools like Sublime Text and Notepad++, and readily accessible via email at sara.hassan@email.com, phone at +20 101 234 5678, or through her online presence at sarahassan.com, GitHub, and LinkedIn.

Corel Draw is a versatile and powerful vector graphics editor, a digital workshop favored by designers like Sara Hassan. It’s a tool built for precision and creativity, enabling the crafting of logos, illustrations, page layouts, and more. While often mentioned alongside industry standards like Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator, Corel Draw distinguishes itself with a focus on vector-based artwork – scalable designs that remain crisp at any size. Hassan skillfully wielded Corel Draw during her tenure at Virtuous Media Point, integrating it with front-end web technologies like HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript to bring client visions to life. It’s a cornerstone of her graphic design skillset, complementing her database knowledge in MySQL 5.0 and broader design expertise, proving its utility across diverse creative projects. Corel Draw isn’t merely software; it’s a conduit for visual communication, a trusted partner in the hands of a skilled designer.

jQuery is a nimble, fast, and feature-rich JavaScript library, a toolkit favored by web artisans like Sara Hassan for breathing life into static webpages. It’s a master of simplifying complex tasks – traversing and manipulating HTML documents, handling events, performing animations, and interacting with AJAX – all with a concise and elegant syntax. While comfortable with foundational languages like C and Java, Hassan consistently turns to jQuery to accelerate web development, seamlessly integrating it with HTML5, CSS3, and Bootstrap. 

From her time at Virtuous Media Point, where she crafted designs for clients, to her internship at e-sense IT Solution, jQuery has been a constant companion in Hassan’s toolkit. She doesn’t merely *use* jQuery; she wields it to build and maintain dynamic administrative panels for diverse web projects – from the vibrant displays of www.nitka.com to the corporate presence of Nagpur united corporation, and the specialized platforms of Mintmetrix.com, Tagline videos, and Smartbadge. It’s a key ingredient in her ability to translate requirements into functional, visually appealing web solutions, a skill honed through participation in the SDLC and Agile methodologies. jQuery, in Hassan’s hands, isn’t just code; it’s a means of bringing digital visions to fruition.

The Project Competition, a vibrant showcase of ingenuity, emerged as a key event within the larger framework of FACE-IT and Innovesta 15. Held at the Priyadarshini Indira Gandhi College of Engineering in Nagpur during 2015, it wasn’t merely a contest, but a platform for aspiring innovators to present their work. It demanded a capable hand to orchestrate – a coordinator who brought structure and energy to the proceedings. The competition itself felt less like a rigid judgment and more like a focused spotlight, drawing attention to projects born from dedicated effort. It existed as a distinct, yet integral, component of both the broader FACE-IT initiative and the multi-faceted Innovesta 15, suggesting a commitment to fostering technical skill and creative problem-solving within the academic environment. It was a proving ground, a stage, and a catalyst for future endeavors, leaving a mark on those who participated and observed.
